behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a handoff correctness check between request-construction code and the API client boundary, not just isolated string logic. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network is not required; a fake transport can assert the outbound payload. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no dependence on model judgment and no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text is only about request content, not real HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that records outgoing payload. Invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the transmitted request contains the full label set (no omissions/reordering loss per contract).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in local validation/normalization logic for returned labels. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: mocking does not hide this failure because input/output mapping is local and deterministic. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not model-quality dependent.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-handling logic with a fixed taxonomy and an out-of-taxonomy label from a synthetic API response; assert output is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism of one local transformation step. No module interaction beyond the normalization function is required, and no real infrastructure or model call is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization twice (or property-style N times) on the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live API calls and consistency tied to provider outputs. Per LLM-DEP, correctness depends on real model behavior/non-determinism; stubs would hide this class of failure. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified by the phrase "live API calls" and consistency across real provider outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real classification calls for the same input, capture raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label changes only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure usually appears at module boundary between token-budget policy and dispatch gate (e.g., builder computes budget but sender still dispatches). REAL-INFRA: real HTTP is unnecessary; in-process wiring can verify "enforced before dispatch." LLM-DEP: no dependence on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use classifier wired to a fake sender plus real budget config/token counter module. Feed over-budget input and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and dispatch behavior matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and provider-reported usage metadata. Per LLM-DEP caution, passing through a real AI API is Agentic by default unless only wrapper plumbing is tested; here the assertion depends on actual model/provider token accounting behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration is disqualified because stubbed usage can always satisfy budget and hide real provider accounting drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run real API classification on representative documents and assert returned usage fields (prompt/total tokens) do not exceed configured budget thresholds.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is retry orchestration across modules (error mapping + backoff + re-dispatch). REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with a deterministic fake API returning rate-limit then success; real network not required for core failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model-judgment dependence, no multi-agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real HTTP timing primitives beyond retry logic.
behaviors.test_description: Wire module with fake API client scripted to return rate-limit signal first, then success. Assert module waits/backs off per policy, retries, and returns successful classification instead of surfacing an error.
